title: eriastrum pluriflorum var. sherman-hoytiae
intro: Eriastrum pluriflorum var. sherman-hoytiae, also known as Sherman's Buckwheat, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Eriastrum pluriflorum plant, a type of flowering perennial that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Sherman's Buckwheat is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Sherman's Buckwheat can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate specific health concerns.
